name: pefile (Windows PE File Parser)

performance.notes: Pure Python parsing; speed depends on PE file size and analysis depth

agent_readiness.notes: Windows PE (Portable Executable) file parser. import pefile. pe = pefile.PE('sample.exe'). pe.OPTIONAL_HEADER.AddressOfEntryPoint. pe.sections. pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT — list of imported DLLs and functions. pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_EXPORT. pe.get_imphash() — import hash for malware clustering. pe.write('patched.exe') for binary patching. Richheader analysis. Overlay detection. Widely used in malware analysis sandboxes (Cuckoo, CAPE). Python-only PE parsing.

gotchas: Windows PE only — no ELF (use pyelftools) or Mach-O support
Malformed PE files may raise exceptions (always use try/except)
Large PE files (packed/obfuscated) can be slow to parse
Import hash changes if import order changes (compilation artifact)
best_when: Malware analysis: import table, entry point, section analysis
Import hash (imphash) generation for malware clustering
avoid_when: ELF binary analysis (use pyelftools or LIEF)
summary: pefile parses Windows PE (Portable Executable) binaries. pefile.PE('sample.exe') gives access to OPTIONAL_HEADER, sections, DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT, DIRECTORY_ENTRY_EXPORT. pe.get_imphash() for malware clustering. Binary patching via pe.write(). Widely used in malware analysis (Cuckoo, CAPE sandbox). Windows PE only — use LIEF for cross-format (PE+ELF+Mach-O) analysis.
